items.final_wording: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

source.title: Emergency treatment of anaphylaxis: Guidelines for healthcare providers
source.population: Adults and children with suspected anaphylaxis.
source.setting: Hospital and out-of-hospital healthcare settings.
source.exclusions: Used only to support anaphylaxis warning documentation associated with rash or urticaria; it does not convert isolated skin findings into anaphylaxis.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

items.rationale: Supports mapped breathing and facial or lip swelling warnings only; skin findings alone are explicitly not treated as anaphylaxis.

added_items.final_wording: Defines pediatric rash use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for pediatric-rash telehealth documentation in Dubai; medication and dose passages were reviewed but deliberately not mapped.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines pediatric rash use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

added_items.rationale: Reviewed to enforce the pediatric population boundary; no content was mapped from scope alone.

added_items.final_wording: Organises pediatric rashes by fever, itching, and neither fever nor itching without proving a diagnosis for an individual child.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for pediatric-rash telehealth documentation in Dubai; medication and dose passages were reviewed but deliberately not mapped.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Organises pediatric rashes by fever, itching, and neither fever nor itching without proving a diagnosis for an individual child.

added_items.rationale: Reviewed to understand fever and itching groupings without assigning a diagnosis; no content was mapped from grouping alone.

added_items.final_wording: Specifies visual assessment plus duration, evolution, distribution, symptoms, family history, recurrence, exposures, medication, impact, travel, allergy, and pet history.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for pediatric-rash telehealth documentation in Dubai; medication and dose passages were reviewed but deliberately not mapped.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies visual assessment plus duration, evolution, distribution, symptoms, family history, recurrence, exposures, medication, impact, travel, allergy, and pet history.

added_items.rationale: Supports mapped pediatric rash history, distribution, morphology, symptom, exposure, medication, impact, and observed skin fields.

added_items.final_wording: Lists emergency and face-to-face review criteria, including confusion, stiff neck, photophobia, persistent or recurrent rash, fever, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for pediatric-rash telehealth documentation in Dubai; medication and dose passages were reviewed but deliberately not mapped.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists emergency and face-to-face review criteria, including confusion, stiff neck, photophobia, persistent or recurrent rash, fever, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty.

added_items.rationale: Supports mapped pediatric fever, pain, immune compromise, uncertainty, worsening, return, and follow-up prompts.

added_items.final_wording: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis
source.population: Children and adults with atopic dermatitis complaints.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth atopic-dermatitis documentation; partial for every eczema or contact-dermatitis subtype.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.

added_items.rationale: Supports mapped eczema or dermatitis terminology and compatible itch, scale, vesicle, excoriation, and erythema fields.

added_items.final_wording: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis
source.population: Children and adults with atopic dermatitis complaints.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth atopic-dermatitis documentation; partial for every eczema or contact-dermatitis subtype.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.

added_items.rationale: Supports mapped fever, painful or rapidly worsening eczema, blister, and infection-warning fields.

added_items.final_wording: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis
source.population: Children and adults with atopic dermatitis complaints.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth atopic-dermatitis documentation; partial for every eczema or contact-dermatitis subtype.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.

added_items.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-documented skin care, trigger, medicine, education, and question fields.

added_items.final_wording: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

source.title: Emergency treatment of anaphylaxis: Guidelines for healthcare providers
source.population: Adults and children with suspected anaphylaxis.
source.setting: Hospital and out-of-hospital healthcare settings.
source.exclusions: Used only to support anaphylaxis warning documentation associated with rash or urticaria; it does not convert isolated skin findings into anaphylaxis.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

added_items.rationale: Supports mapped breathing and facial or lip swelling warnings only; skin findings alone are explicitly not treated as anaphylaxis.
